title: Elon Musk summoned by French prosecutors after 15-month probe
subhead: Paris prosecutors have been investigating a wide range of alleged abuses by X and its owner since January 2025. The billionaire has been summoned for a voluntary interview on Monday.

subhead: Tensions have risen at every stage of the landlord-tenant relationship amid a nationwide housing shortage.

title: The undeniable international success of Languedoc wines
subhead: Appellations from the world's largest wine-growing region sell 40% of their production abroad. Thanks to strong ambassadors, wines from the area are now found in nearly half the countries on the planet.

title: 'Come Closer' by Tomora: When the Chemical Brothers' electronic fever meets Aurora's grace
subhead: British musician Tom Rowlands, one half of the Chemical Brothers, and Norwegian singer Aurora blend their musical worlds on their stunning first joint album.

title: How video games are shaping modern warfare
subhead: Drone piloting with console controllers, first-person shooter games used to recruit soldiers: For the past 60 years, the video game industry and military leadership have been locked in a relationship of mutual dependence.

From a South American frog to a Russian penal colony: How the poison epibatidine killed Navalny
subhead: This extremely powerful neurotoxin is secreted by a frog native to Ecuador. Although Western scientists discovered it in the 19th century, it wasn't synthesized until the 1990s. The neurotoxin appears to have particularly interested the Russian research institute responsible for synthesizing Novichok agents, used in poisonings.
byline: Audrey Lagadec,  Eric Dedier and  Rémi Ducasse

title: A photographic journey into the daily lives of home care aides
subhead: Between fall 2024 and summer 2025, photographer Vincent Gouriou followed several home health aides in Dordogne, southwest France, documenting their often difficult daily routines as they traveled to isolated homes, provided care and offered a listening ear.

title: Saint-Denis Mayor Bally Bagayoko: 'When you win Paris's largest suburb, it gives you a different standing'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, Bally Bagayoko says he can play a role in uniting the left around La France Insoumise leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon for the 2027 French presidential election.
